summary_one_line: Input must carry the 4-byte FOURCC 'CMYK' in the ICC data-colorspace signature field so _cmsLCMScolorSpace dispatches to the cmsSigCmykData case.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The ICC-header data-colorspace signature field (the bytes around offset 0x10-0x13) must equal the exact 4-byte magic 'CMYK' (0x43 0x4D 0x59 0x4B), so the switch on ProfileSpace selects case cmsSigCmykData and takes the blocked true side at cmspcs.c:817.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed places the literal bytes 43 4D 59 4B ('CMYK') in the colorspace region: seed 1/2 at offset 0x10/0x14, seed 3/4/5 with 'CMYK' present; the BYTE DIFF shows W concentrated as 43(C)/4D(M)/59(Y)/4B(K) at offsets 0x10-0x13 and the branch took T=52 in W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offset 0x10-0x13 instead of 'CMYK'; the switch falls through every case to the default arm (cmspcs.c:872, hit only in L), so the cmsSigCmykData true side is never reached (T=0 in L).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the integer/string CMP that tests ProfileSpace against the cmsSigCmykData constant, record both operands in the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes the wanted 'CMYK' magic into the input where the loser's signature bytes were; naive/value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must blindly mutate to the exact 4-byte FOURCC, which they fail to do within budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the cmsSigCmykData case arm of the colorspace switch.
Only the winner exercised the cmsSigCmykData true side; the loser never did.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 817  T=52 F=83  T=0 F=100  case cmsSigCmykData: return  PT_CMYK;

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds contain the literal 'CMYK' bytes in the colorspace field.
At offset 0x10-0x13 winners concentrate on C/M/Y/K while losers carry R/G/B/space.
Loser seeds carry 'RGB ' in the colorspace field instead of 'CMYK'.
The loser falls through to the default arm that the winner never hits.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which substitutes a recorded CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ from their loser only by the I2S technique.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2246 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0102173191ba1d0a` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 9 of the ancestor chain, which the tool flags as characteristic of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current cmplog/vpc build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose cmsSigCmykData-equivalent gate is reached as often by naive/value_profile as by cmplog/vpc (i.e. no I2S advantage) when the gate is an exact 4-byte FOURCC equality compared against an input field.

weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row at depth 9 is a lower-bound I2S signal (the literal I2SRandReplace name is not logged), so I cannot fully exclude that lucky havoc on the 4-byte field also contributed to some winning trials.
